title: mancoa bracteata
intro: Mancoa bracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Calophyllaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. Traditionally, Mancoa bracteata has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its leaves and bark. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent. Mancoa bracteata is often prepared as an infusion or decoction to be consumed as a tea, or used topically as a paste to treat skin conditions and wounds.
